data.title: Inventiva S.A GAAP EPS of -Є1.90, revenue of Є4.5M
data.description:
• Inventiva S.A press release (IVA): FY GAAP EPS of -Є1.90.
  • Revenue of Є4.5M (-51.0% Y/Y).

data.title: M&T Bank authorizes $5B share buyback program
data.description:
• M&T Bank (MTB) on Monday said its board has authorized a share repurchase program of up to $5 billion of its common stock.
  • The new authorization replaces and terminates a previous $4 billion buyback program approved in January 2025.
  • The company said the timing, number of shares repurchased, and transaction terms will be at its discretion and subject to regulatory limits.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Inogen appoints new CFO
data.description:
• Inogen (INGN) named Jason Richardson as chief financial officer effective April 6, 2026, replacing Michael Bourque.
  • Michael Bourque will remain in an advisory role through June 30, 2026, to support the transition. Richardson joins from Baxter International, where he served as chief financial officer.

data.title: Lexeo Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.27 beats by $0.04
data.description:
• Lexeo Therapeutics press release (LXEO): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.27 beats by $0.04.
  •  As of December 31, 2025, cash, cash equivalents, and investments in marketable securities were $246.6 million, which Lexeo believes will be sufficient to fund operations into 2028.

data.title: Annexon Biosciences GAAP EPS of -$0.28 beats by $0.04
data.description:
• Annexon Biosciences press release (ANNX): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.28 beats by $0.04.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments were $238.3 million as of December 31, 2025, including $86.3 million in gross proceeds from a November 2025 public offering. Based on focused investments in its lead late-stage programs, Annexon expects to fund operations and anticipated milestones into the second half of 2027.

data.title: Ukraine eyes drone export boom as Middle East conflicts highlight demand
data.description:
Ukraine is positioning its battle-tested drone defense technology for export as conflicts in the Middle East expose growing vulnerabilities to aerial attacks, Reuters reported Monday.

President Volodymyr Zelenskiy recently traveled across Gulf nations to explore defense partnerships, promoting Ukraine’s expertise in countering drone threats. Rather than simply selling equipment, he emphasized that Ukraine offers a full operational approach that combines technology, training and strategy into an integrated defense system.

Kyiv has already signed preliminary cooperation agreements with Saudi Arabia and Qatar, with discussions ongoing with the United Arab Emirates. Ukrainian officials have also stressed that any arms exports must be coordinated at the government level, discouraging private companies from acting independently.

Defense firms are eager to expand abroad. Industry leaders say interest from the Gulf has surged as recent conflicts demonstrated how inexpensive attack drones can overwhelm traditional defenses. One executive described companies as effectively waiting on approval to move forward with deals.

Ukraine’s rapid innovation stems from years of defending against large-scale drone attacks from Russia, often involving hundreds of drones in a single night. This pressure has driven the development of relatively low-cost interceptor drones designed to neutralize incoming threats before impact.

The country’s defense sector believes exports could reach billions of dollars annually, potentially becoming a major pillar of postwar economic recovery. However, industry representatives warn that delays in government approvals could cost Ukraine a critical window of opportunity as global demand accelerates.

Among the technologies drawing attention are sea-based drones capable of launching interceptors, originally used by Ukraine to challenge Russian naval forces. These systems are now being adapted for broader air defense roles, including coastal protection.

Despite strong interest, experts caution that deploying such systems is complex. Effective use requires trained operators, coordinated radar networks and integration across multiple units. One defense official noted that while Gulf countries could develop these capabilities within months, the urgency of current threats leaves little margin for delay.

As global conflicts reshape defense priorities, Ukraine is racing to turn wartime innovation into a competitive export industry before the moment passes, Reuters reported.

data.title: Judge OK's government approval of Ioneer's Nevada lithium mine
data.description:
Ioneer (IONR) up 25.4% in late trading Monday after a U.S. District Judge ruled that U.S. Interior Department regulators during the Biden administration sufficiently analyzed how the company's planned Rhyolite Ridge lithium and boron mine project in Nevada could affect a rare wildflower that the company has promised to protect.

According to Reuters, the judge rejected claims by environmentalists that the U.S. ​government improperly approved Ioneer's (IONR) project, which is expected to become one of North ⁠America's largest sources of lithium aand a key supplier to automaker Ford and others.

The Center for Biological ​Diversity environmental group, which filed suit in a bid to halt the project, had argued the mine could drive the flower to extinction ​and harm groundwater, air quality, cultural resources and wildlife habitats.

data.title: Perfect Moment raises $12M in funding with $10M credit facility
data.description:
• Perfect Moment (PMNT) raised $12M in funding, including a $10M revolving credit facility from Krane Capital ($4 million) and X3 Higher Moment Fund ($6 million), to support its growth.
  • The credit facility has a 24-month loan with 12% interest.
  • Krane Capital will invest an additional $2M by purchasing ~6.06M shares at $0.33 each, and the gross proceeds will be used to strengthen the company’s balance sheet and liquidity.
  • The credit facility closed on March 30, 2026, while the $2M equity investment will close within the next month.

data.title: Ovintiv cut at Citi, seeing declining free cash flow yields given less oil leverage
data.description:
Ovintiv (OVV) down 2.6% in Monday's trading as Citi downgraded shares of the oil and gas E&P company to Neutral from Buy with a $62 price target, raised from $52, saying its portfolio—particularly the Montney—is underappreciated and advantaged due to its gas weighting at 50% of production but has been largely realigned, and the stock is trading high relative to peers.

After shares rose more than 50% YTD and hit $60, Ovintiv (OVV) presents estimates of EV/DACF multiples of ~5.5x and ~6.0 in 2026 and 2027, respectively, in line with companies of similar scale, Citi analyst Scott Gruber said, but free cash flow is worse given less oil leverage with FCF yields seen at ~5.9% and 4.6% in 2026 and 2027, blending to ~5.3% in a low-$70s WTI
scenario scenario—below some larger-cap peers, and thus the stock downgrade.

While commending Ovintiv (OVV) on streamlining its portfolio down to two core basins and deleveraging the balance sheet, and noting the company retains a long inventory position—especially in Canada, with 20 years of condensate-rich inventory—and is a leader in shale operations both in terms of technique and technology deployment, Gruber noted the shares have climbed to $60, broadly in line with his fair value estimate.

While seeing longer-term prospects for greater oil and gas exports out of Canada, the market likely remains well supplied in the near term, Gruber said; in addition, Ovintiv (OVV) could be aggressive on buybacks given the 75% cash return pledge for 2026, but "we view this as more of a stock cushion vs. an alpha driver now that the stock has hit $60," the analyst wrote.
